title: Die Chronik der Sperlingsgasse
summaries: "Die Chronik der Sperlingsgasse" by Wilhelm Raabe is a novel published in 1857. An elderly man named Johannes Wachholder chronicles his life and the lives of residents in a Berlin alley over one winter and spring. Through diary-like entries, he recalls his unfulfilled love for Marie, his role as guardian to her orphaned daughter, and the intertwined fates of families bound by secrets, tragedy, and redemption across generations in 1850s Berlin.
